hadiths.ar:
663   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :   «
ق َّق َّذْ ذَّج َّلَّ وَّق ْث ُّ إَّلْعِس َّاءِ إَّلْأ ج ِزَّة ِ إ ِلَّى إِن ْي ِصَّاف ِ إَّلل َّي ْلِ .
hadiths.en:
Abu Usama Zayd al-Shahham said: "One time, I climbed the mountain of Abu 
Qubais, and the people were praying Maghrib. I saw that the sun had not completely 
set; it had only disappeared behind the mountain from the view of the people.  
I met Abu Abdullah {a.s} Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} and informed him of 
this.  
Imam {a.s} said to me: 'Why did you do that? It was not a good action. You should 
perform the Maghrib prayer when you can no longer see the sun behind the mountain, 
whether it has set or gone down, unless it is obscured by a cloud or darkness that 
covers it.  
What concerns you is your eastern horizon and western horizon, and people are not 
required to investigate further.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 220 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 80 • Tahdhib Al-
Ahkam, Part 2, p. 264 • Al -Istibsar, Part 1, p. 266 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 259 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 198 • 
Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 57 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 662
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٢

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"When the sun sets, it becomes permissible to break the fast (if one is fasting) and the 
Maghrib prayer becomes obligatory. Once you have performed the Maghrib prayer, the 
time for the Isha (night) prayer begins and extends until midnight."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 221 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 276 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
179 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 184 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 663
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٣

hadiths.ar:
665  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
عِلِّي ِّي ن َّ ق َّا ِن ْ صَّل َّى إ َّرْن َّعاً كُّي ِن َّث ْ لَّهُّ جَّح  َّه   مَّن ْزُّورَّة .   
ووق ث  إلق ح ز جي ن  ي عي زض  إلق ح ز وي ص ى  جسن ا  وي ي ح لل إلصن ح إلسماء وي كون  كالق ن اطى  إ و مي ل ن هز سورإء . ومن  صلى  
إلع ذإة  ق ى  إ ول وق ي ها إ ي ن ي ث  له مزي ي ن ، إ ي ن ي ها ملان كه  إللي ل وملان كه  إلن هار، ومن  صلاها ق ى  إ ج ز وق ي ها إ ي ن ي ث  له مزة  وإجذة ، 
ق ال إلله عز  وج ل: " ۝ إ َّق ِمِ إ لص َّلَّوٰة َّ لِذُّلُّوك ِ إ لس  َّمْسِ إ ِلَّىٰ ع َّسَّق ِ إ ل َّي ْلِ وَّق ُّزْءَّإن َّ إ لْق َّح ْزِ ۖ إ ِن  َّ ق ُّزْءَّإن َّ إ لْق َّح ْزِ كَّان َّ مَّس ْهُّو ذًًۭإ  ۝" ي عن ى   
إ ن ه ي س هذها ملان كه  إللي ل وملان كه  إلن هار.
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"A designated angel says, 'Whoever neglects the Isha prayer until the middle of the 
night, may Allah {SWT} never allow his eyes to sleep.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 221 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 279 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
184 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 214 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 664
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٤

hadiths.ar:
666  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
وَّ إَّلْحَّص َّزِ وَّإجِذ  وَّ هُّوَّ مِن َّ إَّلْمُّص َّي  َّق ِ وَّ صَّلاَّة ُّ إَّلْعَّصْزِ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ ق ِى  وَّق ْث ِ إَّلْأ ُّولَّى ق ِى  سَّان ِزِ إَّلْأ َّي  َّامِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever prays Maghrib and then follows it immediately with the post -prayer 
(Ta'qeeb) supplication without speaking, and performs two units of prayer (Rak'ahs), 
they are recorded for him among those in 'Illiyyīn (the highest rank in Paradise). If he 
prays four units (Rak'ahs), it will be written for him as an accepted pilgrimage (Hajj 
Mabroor)."  
[AL SADUQ] 
The time of Fajr is when the light of dawn spreads and becomes visible, illuminating 
the sky such that it appears like the fabric of qibatis (a thin, white cloth) or like the River 
of Surā’ (a bright river).  
Whoever prays the morning prayer at its earliest time will have it recorded twice  -  once 
by the angels of the night and once by the angels of the day. Whoever prays it at its 
later time will have it recorded once. Allah {SWT}, Exalted and Glorious, says: 
'Establish the prayer at the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night, and the 
recitation of the dawn. Verily, the recitation of the dawn is witnessed ' (Qur'an 17:78), 
meaning that it is witnessed by both the angels of the night and the angels of the day.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Thawab al -A’mal wa Iqab al -A’mal, Part 1, p. 45 • Al -
Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 585 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 113 • Al-Waezin, volume 2, page 317 • Falah 
Al-Sael, Part 1, p. 232 • Meftah al-Falah, Part 1, p. 237 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 197 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 488 
• Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 83, p. 100 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 665
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٥

hadiths.ar:
667 - وَّ رَّوَّى إ ِسْمَّاعِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ رَّن َّاحٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ي َّزَّى إ َّن  َّك َّ ق ِى  وَّق ْث ٍ وَّ 
لَّمْ ي َّذْج ُّلِ إَّلْوَّق ْث ُّ ق َّذَّج َّلَّ إَّلْوَّق ْث ُّ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّق َّذْ إ َّج ْز َّإ َّب ْ عَّن ْك َّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"The time for the Jumu’ah prayer on the day of Jumu'ah (Jumu’ah) is at the hour when 
the sun begins to decline (from its zenith), and its timing is the same in both travel and 
residence. It is a restricted (fixed) time. The 'Asr (afternoon) prayer on the d ay of 
Jumu'ah is at the time of the Dhuhr (noon) prayer on other days."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 222 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 412 • Al -
Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1109 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 125 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 7, p. 318 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 666
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٦

hadiths.ar:
668  - وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ ن ْن مِهْزَّإن َّ : عَّن ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ن ِالل َّي ْلِ وَّ إَّلن  َّهَّارِ إ ِذ َّإ لَّمْ ي ُّزَّ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ وَّ إَّلْق َّمَّزُّ وَّ لأَّ إَّلن  ُّح ُّومُّ ق َّق َّال 
رَّإ ْي َّك َّ وَّ ي َّعَّم َّذُّ إَّلْق ِن ْلَّه َّ ن ِح ُّهْذِك َّ .
hadiths.en:
Ismail ibn Rabah narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -who said:  
"If you begin praying while you believe that it is within the (correct) time, and the time 
for the prayer enters while you are in the middle of the prayer, then it suffices for you 
(prayer is valid and accepted)."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 286 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 222 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 35 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 2, p. 141 • Awaali al -La’aali, Part 3, p. 71 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 308 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 206 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 667
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٧

hadiths.ar:
669   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلْق َّز َّإءُّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ لَّهُّ رَّج ُّل  مِن ْ إ َّصْحَّان ِن َّا إ ِن  َّهُّ رُّن  َّمَّا إِس ْي َّن َّهَّ عَّلَّي ْن َّا  
إَّلْوَّق ْث ُّ ق ِى  ي َّوْمِ ع َّي ْمٍ ق َّق َّال ي ُّوك 
إِرْي َّق َّعَّث ْ  إ َّصْوَّإي ُّهَّا وَّ ي َّح َّاوَّن َّث ْ ق َّعِن ْذَّ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّصَّلِّ .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a ibn Mehran asked Him about praying during the day or night when 
neither the sun, the moon, nor the stars are visible.  
Imam {a.s} replied: "You exert your own judgment and strive to determine the Qiblah 
(direction of prayer) to the best of your ability." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 284 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 222 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 46 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 46 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 2, p. 255 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 295 
• Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 295 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 255 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 548 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 308 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 668
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٨

hadiths.ar:
670  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْمُّح ْي َّارِ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: إ ِن ِّى  مُّو َّذ ِّن   ق َّا ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ ي َّوْم ع َّي ْمٍ لَّمْ إ َّعْزِف ِ إَّلْوَّق ْث َّ ق َّق َّالَّ 
 ومن  صلى لع ي ز إلق ن له  ق ى  ي وم ع ي م ي م علم، ق ا ن  كان  ق ى  وق ث  ق لي عذ، وإ ن  كان  ق ذ مص ى إلوق ث  ق لا إ عاذة  علي ه وجسن ه 
إج ي هاذة.
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} Al-Farra' that a man from among their 
companions said to him: "Sometimes the time of prayer becomes unclear for us on a 
cloudy day."  
Imam said: "Do you know these birds that you have in Iraq, which are called roosters 
(duyook)?"  
The man replied: "Yes."  
He {a.s} said: "When their voices rise and they begin to respond to each other, then at 
that time, pray." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 222 • Al -Sarayer, Part 3, p. 613 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 
80, p. 44  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
«.

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 669
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٦٩

hadiths.en:
Al-Hussein ibn al-Mukhtar narrated from, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq 
{a.s}, that He (ibn al-Mukhtar) said: "I am the muezzin (caller of prayer). On a cloudy day, 
when I cannot ascertain the time (for prayer),  
Imam {a.s} said: 'When the rooster crows three consecutive times, then the sun has 
passed its zenith and the time for prayer has entered.'" 
[AL SADUQ] 
Additionally, it is mentioned that if someone prays facing the wrong direction (not 
toward the Qibla) on a cloudy day and later realizes their mistake: if they prayed within 
the correct prayer time, they should repeat the prayer. However, if the time has 
passed, they are not obligated to repeat it, as their effort was based on due diligence.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 223 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 255 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 670
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٧٠

hadiths.ar:
672  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ وَّهْث ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  َّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
وَّ إ لِهِ ق ِى  إَّلْحَّزِّ ق ِى  صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلط  ُّهْزِ ق َّي َّق ُّولُّ لَّهُّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ .    
ق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب : ي عن ى  عح ل عح ل وإ ج ذ  ذ لك  من  إلي ن زي ذ.
hadiths.en:
Abu Ja'far, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, said:  
"It is more beloved to me to pray after the time (for prayer) has passed than to pray 
while I am in doubt about whether it is the correct time or before the time (for prayer) 
has entered." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 223 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 307 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 671
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٧١

hadiths.en:
Muawiya ibn Wahb narrated from Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"The muezzin would come to The Prophet {saws} during the intense heat at the time of 
the Dhuhr prayer, and the Messenger of Allah {saws} would tell him, 'Cool down, cool 
down (abride abride).'"  
[AL SADUQ] 
The compiler of this book explains: It means "hasten, hasten," and this is derived from 
the term for cooling (referring to delaying the prayer slightly to avoid extreme heat).  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 223 • Al -Arba‘ūn 
Ḥadīthan (Al-Shahīd al-Awwal) Vol. 1, p. 46 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 248 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 142 • Wasail 
Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 247 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 15

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 32, H. 672
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٢، ح ٦٧٢

hadiths.chapter: 33. KNOWING THE DECLINE OF THE SUN
hadiths.ar:
674  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
إ َّصَّان ِغَّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ ن َّق َّضَّ إَّلط ِّل ُّ جَّي  َّى ي َّن ْلُّغ َّ ع َّاي َّي َّهُّ ي ُّم َّ ر َّإذَّ ق َّق َّذْ ر َّإلَّث ِ إَّلس  َّمْسُّ وَّ ي ُّق َّي  َّح  إ َّن ْوَّإب ُّ إَّلس َّمَّا ءِ وَّ ي َّهُّث  ُّ  
إَّلزِّي َّاحُّ وَّ ي ُّق ْص َّى إَّلْحَّوَّإن ِح ُّ إَّلْعِط َّامُّ .

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 33, H. 673
reference.chapter: KNOWING THE DECLINE OF THE SUN
reference.location: Volume 1 › KNOWING THE DECLINE OF THE SUN
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٣، ح ٦٧٣

hadiths.chapter: 33. KNOWING THE DECLINE OF THE SUN
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The indication of the sun's zenith (midday) is to take a stick that is one cubit and four 
fingers in length and place four fingers into the ground (embedding the stick partially). 
When the shadow shortens to its minimum point and then begins to lengthen, the sun 
has passed its zenith. At this point, the gates of the heavens open, the winds blow, and 
great needs are fulfilled." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 224 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 249 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 164

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 33, H. 674
reference.chapter: KNOWING THE DECLINE OF THE SUN
reference.location: Volume 1 › KNOWING THE DECLINE OF THE SUN
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٣، ح ٦٧٤

hadiths.ar:
676   -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :   عَّن ِ إَّلس  َّمْسِ كَّي ْف َّ ي َّزْكُّذُّ كُّل َّ ي َّوْمٍ وَّ لأَّ ي َّكُّون ُّ لَّهَّا ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ رُّكُّوذ  ق َّال 
إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ج َّعَّلَّ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ إ َّص ْي َّق َّ إَّلْأ َّي  َّامِ   ق َّال   ي ُّعَّذ ِّب ُّ إَّلْمُّس ْزِكِي ن َّ 
ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلْي َّوْمِ لِحُّزْمَّي ِهِ عِن ْذَّةُّ .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} stillness 
of the sun.  
Imam {a.s} said: "O’ Muhammad, how small is your form and how challenging is your 
question! And indeed, you are worthy of an answer.  
When the sun rises, seventy thousand angels pull it, and for each of its rays, five 
thousand angels are responsible for both pulling and pushing it.  
When it reaches the sky and passes the 'arc,' the angel of light flips it from back to front 
so that what was facing the earth turns towards the sky, and its rays reach the 
boundaries of the Throne. At this moment, the angels exclaim,  
'Glory be to Allah {SWT}, and there is no deity but Allah {SWT}, and all praise is due to Allah 
{SWT}, who has not taken a consort nor a son, and who has no partner in His dominion, 
nor does He need a protector out of weakness, so glorify Him with great glorification.'" 
Muhammad then asked: "Shall I maintain this supplication at the sun's zenith?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, preserve it as you would preserve your own eyes, for when the sun 
passes its zenith, the angels remain behind it, glorifying Allah {SWT} in the sky until it 
sets”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 225 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 252 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 
165 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 55, p. 167 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 34, H. 675
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٤، ح ٦٧٥

hadiths.ar:
677  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ جَّزِي ز ِ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: كُّن ْث ُّ عِن ْذَّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّسَّا َّلَّه  رَّج ُّل  ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ ج ُّعِلْث ُّ  
ق ِذَّإك َّ إ ِن  َّ إَّلس  َّمْسَّ ي َّن ْق َّض  ُّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّزْكُّذُّ سَّاعَّه ً مِن ْ ق َّن ْلِ إ َّن ْ ي َّز ُّولَّ ق َّق َّال  ي ُّو َّإمِزُّ إ َّ ي َّز ُّولُّ إ َّوْ لأَّ ي َّز ُّولُّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the sun’s stillness 
(or 'pause') every day and why there is no such stillness for it on Jumu’ah.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Because Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, made Jumu’ah the most 
constricted (or significant) of the days."  
It was then asked: "And why did He make it the most constricted of days?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Because on that day He does not punish the polytheists, due to the 
sanctity it holds with Him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 225 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1084 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 55, 
p. 168 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 34, H. 676
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٤، ح ٦٧٦

hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Hariz ibn Abdullah that He said: I was with Abu 'Abdillah 
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} when a man asked him,  
"May I be sacrificed for you, the sun seems to descend (move downward) and then 
becomes still for a moment before it reaches its zenith."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It consults whether it should continue or not”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 251 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 55, p. 171

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 34, H. 677
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٣٤، ح ٦٧٧

hadiths.chapter: 35. THE RECOGNITION OF THE END OF THE NIGHT
hadiths.en:
Umar ibn Hanthala asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} saying:  
"We recognize the zenith (midday) of the sun during the day; how can we recognize the 
zenith during the night?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "The night has a zenith just like the zenith of the sun."  
Umar then asked: "And by what means do we recognize it?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "By the stars when they descend”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 227 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Vol. 4, p. 273 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 137

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 35, H. 678
reference.chapter: THE RECOGNITION OF THE END OF THE NIGHT

hadiths.chapter: 36. THE PRAYER OF THE MESSENGER, AT THE TIME OF
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {saws} would not pray anything during the day until the sun had 
passed its zenith. When it did, he would pray eight rak'ahs, known as the prayer of the 
'Awwabin (the devoted). At that moment, the gates of the heavens are opened, prayers 
are answered, the winds blow, and Allah {SWT} looks upon His creation.  
When the shadow lengthened by a cubit, he would pray Dhuhr (noon) prayer in four 
rak'ahs, followed by two rak'ahs, then two additional rak'ahs.  
Then, he would pray 'Asr (afternoon) prayer in four rak'ahs once the shadow 
lengthened by another cubit. After 'Asr, he would not pray anything until the sun 
inclined (set).  
When it inclined, that is, set, he would pray Maghrib (sunset) prayer in three rak'ahs, 
followed by four rak'ahs. Then, he would not pray until the redness of the twilight 
disappeared.  
When the twilight disappeared, he would pray 'Isha' prayer and then retire to his bed, 
praying nothing further until the middle of the night had passed.  
When midnight passed, he would pray eight rak'ahs, followed by three rak'ahs of Witr 
(night prayer), reciting the Surat al-Fatiha and 'Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad' (Surah Al-Ikhlas) 
in them. He would separate the three rak'ahs with a taslim (salutation), speak or fulfil 
any needs, and not leave place of prayer until he completed third rak'ah with Qunoot 
(supplication) before bowing.  
He would conclude with two rak'ahs of Fajr (pre-dawn prayer) slightly before, at, or just 
after dawn, and then he would pray two rak'ahs of Subh (morning prayer) when the 
dawn light brightened, completing his routine.  
This was the prayer practice of the Messenger of Allah {saws} until Allah {SWT} took him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 227 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 
7, p. 312 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 61 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 80, p. 136

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 36, H. 679

hadiths.chapter: 37. THE VIRTUE OF MOSQUES, THEIR SANCTITY AND THE
hadiths.ar:
681   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو جَّمْز َّة َّ إَّلي  ُّمَّالِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال -   «
مَّكْي ُّون َّه ً ق َّن ِلَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ن ِهَّا مِن ْهُّ كُّل َّ صَّلاَّة ٍ صَّلا َّهَّا مُّن ْذ ُّ ي َّوْمَّ وَّج َّن َّث ْ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ وَّ كُّل َّ صَّلاَّة ٍ   ي ُّصَّلِّي هَّا إ ِلَّى إ َّن ْ ي َّمُّوب
hadiths.en:
Khalid ibn Maad al-Qalanisi narrated from Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"Makkah is the sanctuary of Allah {SWT}, the sanctuary of His Messenger, and the 
sanctuary of Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}. A prayer performed therein is equivalent to one 
hundred thousand prayers, and one dirham spent there is equivalent to one hundred 
thousand dirhams.  
Madinah is the sanctuary of Allah {SWT}, the sanctuary of His Messenger, and the 
sanctuary of Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}. A prayer performed therein is equivalent to ten 
thousand prayers, and one dirham spent there is equivalent to ten thousand dirhams.  
Kufa is the sanctuary of Allah {SWT}, the sanctuary of His Messenger, and the sanctuary 
of Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}. A prayer performed therein is equivalent to one thousand 
prayers." Imam {a.s} remained silent about the dirham (spent there). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 228 • Al-Mazar Volume 1, page 5 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, 
Vol. 6, p. 31 • The Great Shrine, Part 1, p. 114 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 256 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 37, H. 680

hadiths.chapter: 37. THE VIRTUE OF MOSQUES, THEIR SANCTITY AND THE
hadiths.ar:
682   -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ ق ِى  إَّلْمَّسْح ِذِ إَّلْحَّزَّإمِ ي َّعْذِلُّ إ َّلْف َّ صَّلاَّة ٍ ق ِى  مَّسْح ِذِى .
hadiths.en:
Abu Hamza al -Thumali narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
that He said:  
"Whoever performs an obligatory prayer in the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al -Haram), 
Allah {SWT} accepts it as equivalent to every prayer they have performed since the day 
prayer became obligatory upon them, and every prayer they will perform until they 
die." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 228 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 47 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 37, H. 681
